subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025/2026, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Moroccan official (HCP, Bank Al-Maghrib) + independent/French-language press, weighting external monitors over state messaging on rights given documented media pressure.
A stable monarchy where the king holds real power; the dirham runs a managed peg-basket. Rights/press scores lean on RSF/HRW/Amnesty rather than state framing.

Standout features: genuine economic diversification (autos, aerospace, phosphates, solar), strong infrastructure, and Western alignment, against constrained rights/press, structural water/seismic risk, and an unresolved territorial dispute. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone.

economic.notes: Genuine industrial diversification, a credible managed-peg central bank, and low inflation.

fiscal_state.notes: Moderate debt ~70% GDP with a narrowing deficit, against a large World-Cup-2030 and post-quake investment surge. (IMF Art IV 2025/2026)

monetary_independence.notes: A managed peg-basket with a gradual move toward inflation targeting; the central bank is credible. (Bank Al-Maghrib)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dirham is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

banking_stability.notes: Resilient and well-capitalised with pan-African expansion, with NPL/concentration flags. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Genuine diversification - autos (a major export hub), aerospace, phosphates (vast reserves), solar, and tourism - with a mild phosphate-concentration note. (Automotive Logistics; IMF)

institutional.notes: Credible statistics and reform delivery, against a king-dominated rule of law and constrained press.

rule_of_law.notes: A king-dominated system with partial judicial independence and corruption. (WJP; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: The planning agency is credible and IMF-engaged. (HCP)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Technocratic pockets with ambitious reform delivery, uneven locally. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Constrained - a poor RSF standing, fabricated-charge prosecutions, and spyware concerns. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: A stable but not fully democratic monarchy, with constrained liberties and a favourable youth window.

political_stability.notes: A stable monarchy where the king holds real power and parliament is limited - durable but not a full democracy. (EIU; V-Dem)

civil_liberties.notes: Red lines on the monarchy, Islam, and Western Sahara, though more open than regional peers. (Amnesty; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A transit/destination for sub-Saharan migration with regularisation waves but documented raids and no easy citizenship. (HRW; MPI)

social_cohesion.notes: Relatively cohesive, offset by inequality and rural-urban and Amazigh grievances. (observed)

demographics.notes: A favourable youth window now, with fertility falling and ageing ahead. (HCP census 2024)

geopolitical.notes: A stable, Western-aligned partner, against the unresolved Western Sahara dispute.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable US/EU/Gulf partner, normalised with Israel, with rising Western backing on Western Sahara. (Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: The unresolved Western Sahara dispute and severed Algeria ties, low-intensity. (UN; Al Jazeera)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: A managed, partly-open capital account; unsanctioned. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Strong infrastructure and a health-coverage expansion, against structural water scarcity and a seismic tail.

climate.notes: Severe multi-year drought, water stress, and heat, but among Africa's stronger readiness/adaptation programs. (ND-GAIN; WB MENA)

healthcare.notes: A major universal-health reform expanding coverage, with uneven quality and access. (HCP; reform docs)

infrastructure.notes: A relative strength - a major port, high-speed rail, roads, and a World-Cup build. (Tanger Med)

crime_safety.notes: Generally safe, with moderate petty crime. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: A catastrophe-insurance scheme paid out post-quake via sovereign reinsurance; seismic/flood exposure remains. (World Bank)

summary: A stable monarchy with a genuinely diversified economy - autos, aerospace, phosphates, solar, and tourism - a credible managed-peg central bank, low inflation, strong and improving infrastructure, and a Western-aligned foreign policy with growing backing on Western Sahara. The offsets are a king-dominated rule of law with constrained press freedom (prosecutions and spyware concerns), limited political rights with red lines on the monarchy and Islam, and a structural water-scarcity and seismic-hazard tail. A universal-health reform is expanding coverage from an uneven base.
